title: VEAL CHOP Mnemonic
description: Used for fetal heart rate monitoring: Variable = Cord compression, Early = Head compression, Accelerations = Okay, Late = Placental insufficiency.

title: GTPAL Obstetric History
description: Records reproductive history: Gravida (total pregnancies), Term births (>37 weeks), Preterm births (20-36 weeks), Abortions (<20 weeks), and Living children.

title: APGAR Scoring System
description: Evaluates newborn health at 1 and 5 minutes based on Activity, Pulse, Grimace, Appearance, and Respiration. Scores of 7-10 indicate good overall health.

title: The 4 Stages of Labor
description: Stage 1: Onset to full cervical dilation (10 cm). Stage 2: Full dilation to birth. Stage 3: Birth to placental expulsion. Stage 4: First 4 hours of postpartum stabilization.

title: Postpartum Hemorrhage (PPH) Metrics
description: PPH is defined as a blood loss of more than 500 mL following a vaginal delivery, or more than 1,000 mL following a cesarean delivery within 24 hours.

title: Naegele's Rule
description: A formula to estimate the delivery date: Count backward 3 months from the first day of the woman's last menstrual period, then add 7 days.

title: Normal Fetal Heart Rate
description: A healthy, normal fetal heart rate ranges between 120 and 160 beats per minute.
